512	\section{The dimension-shift step}
513	
514	The induction driving the comparison theorem rests on a single short-exact-sequence
515	calculation: cutting a resolution into short exact sequences and reading off the
516	long exact sequence of right-derived functors. The latter is not available off the
517	shelf at the level of the derived-functor objects \((R^k G)\); we build it from the
518	complex-level homology long exact sequence
519	(Lemma~\ref{lem:homology_long_exact_sequence}) via the horseshoe lift
520	(Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_resolution_of_ses}). We first record the shift at the
521	\emph{resolution} level — where the horseshoe lift directly supplies the
522	degreewise-split short exact sequence of injective resolutions — and then specialize
523	to objects.
524	
525	\begin{lemma}\leanok
526	[Resolution-level dimension shift]
527	  \label{lem:right_derived_shift_split_resolution}
528	  \lean{CategoryTheory.Functor.rightDerivedShiftIsoOfSplitResolutionSES}
529	  \uses{def:right_acyclic, lem:right_derived_injective_resolution,
530	    lem:homology_long_exact_sequence}
531	  Let \(G : \mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{B}\) be additive and let
532	  \(0 \to A \to J \to Z \to 0\) be a short exact sequence with \(J\) right-\(G\)-acyclic,
533	  lifted to a \emph{degreewise-split} short exact sequence of injective resolutions
534	  \(0 \to I_A \to I_J \to I_Z \to 0\) (chain maps \(\varphi, \psi\) with
535	  \(\psi \circ \varphi = 0\) and a splitting of \(0 \to I_A^n \to I_J^n \to I_Z^n \to 0\)
536	  in each degree \(n\)). Then for every \(k \geq 0\) the connecting map of the homology
537	  long exact sequence of \(G(I_\bullet)\) is an isomorphism
538	  \[
539	    (R^{k+1} G)(Z) \;\cong\; (R^{k+2} G)(A).
540	  \]
541	\end{lemma}
542	
543	\begin{proof}
544	  \uses{def:right_acyclic, lem:right_derived_injective_resolution,
545	    lem:homology_long_exact_sequence}
546	    \leanok
547	  Applying \(G\) degreewise to the degreewise-split sequence preserves each split
548	  degree, yielding a short exact sequence of cochain complexes
549	  \(0 \to G(I_A) \to G(I_J) \to G(I_Z) \to 0\) in \(\mathcal{B}\). Its homology long
550	  exact sequence (Lemma~\ref{lem:homology_long_exact_sequence}) has connecting maps
551	  \(\delta^m : H^m(G(I_Z)) \to H^{m+1}(G(I_A))\); transport these across the
552	  injective-resolution computation \(H^m(G(I_-)) \cong (R^m G)(-)\)
553	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:right_derived_injective_resolution}). For \(m = k+1 \geq 1\) the two
554	  homology objects flanking \(\delta^m\) in the long exact sequence both sit on the
555	  middle complex \(G(I_J)\), namely \(H^{k+1}(G(I_J))\) and \(H^{k+2}(G(I_J))\); both
556	  vanish because \(J\) is right-\(G\)-acyclic (Definition~\ref{def:right_acyclic}).
557	  With both neighbours zero, \(\delta^{k+1}\) is an isomorphism, giving
558	  \((R^{k+1} G)(Z) \cong (R^{k+2} G)(A)\).
559	\end{proof}

607	% The proof below constructs the same sequence at the level of right-derived-functor

612	\begin{proof}
613	  \uses{def:right_acyclic, lem:right_derived_zero_iso_self,
614	    lem:right_derived_injective_resolution, lem:homology_long_exact_sequence,

617	  We first construct the long exact sequence of right-derived functors associated
618	  to \(0 \to A \to J \to Z \to 0\). In the classical treatment this sequence is
619	  supplied by the \(\delta\)-functor structure of \(\{R^k G\}\) on the derived
620	  category; here it is built directly from the complex-level homology long exact
621	  sequence via a horseshoe lift.
622	
623	  \emph{Building the long exact sequence.} By the horseshoe lift
624	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_resolution_of_ses}) choose injective resolutions
625	  \(I_A, I_J, I_Z\) of \(A, J, Z\) fitting into a short exact sequence of cochain
626	  complexes
627	  \[
628	    0 \longrightarrow I_A \longrightarrow I_J \longrightarrow I_Z \longrightarrow 0
629	  \]
630	  that is degreewise split. Apply \(G\) degreewise. Because each degree
631	  \(0 \to I_A^n \to I_J^n \to I_Z^n \to 0\) is a \emph{split} short exact sequence
632	  (so \(I_J^n \cong I_A^n \oplus I_Z^n\) compatibly with the maps), the additive
633	  functor \(G\) carries it to a split short exact sequence
634	  \(0 \to G(I_A^n) \to G(I_J^n) \to G(I_Z^n) \to 0\) --- this is where degreewise
635	  splitness is essential, since \(G\) is not assumed exact and would not in general
636	  preserve a non-split short exact sequence. Hence
637	  \[
638	    0 \longrightarrow G(I_A) \longrightarrow G(I_J) \longrightarrow G(I_Z)
639	      \longrightarrow 0
640	  \]
641	  is a short exact sequence of cochain complexes in \(\mathcal{B}\). The
642	  complex-level homology long exact sequence
643	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:homology_long_exact_sequence}) applied to it reads
644	  \[
645	    \cdots \to H^k\bigl(G(I_J)\bigr) \to H^k\bigl(G(I_Z)\bigr)
646	      \xrightarrow{\ \delta^k\ } H^{k+1}\bigl(G(I_A)\bigr)
647	      \to H^{k+1}\bigl(G(I_J)\bigr) \to \cdots .
648	  \]
649	  By Lemma~\ref{lem:right_derived_injective_resolution} the homology of each applied
650	  resolution computes the right-derived functor:
651	  \(H^k(G(I_A)) \cong (R^k G)(A)\), \(H^k(G(I_J)) \cong (R^k G)(J)\) and
652	  \(H^k(G(I_Z)) \cong (R^k G)(Z)\), naturally in the maps of the sequence. Transporting
653	  the homology long exact sequence across these isomorphisms gives the long exact
654	  sequence of right-derived functors
655	  \[
656	    \cdots \to (R^k G)(J) \to (R^k G)(Z)
657	      \xrightarrow{\ \delta^k\ } (R^{k+1} G)(A) \to (R^{k+1} G)(J) \to \cdots,
658	  \]
659	  and, using \(R^0 G \cong G\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:right_derived_zero_iso_self}) at the
660	  bottom, its low-degree initial segment
661	  \[
662	    0 \to G(A) \to G(J) \to G(Z)
663	      \xrightarrow{\ \delta^0\ } (R^1 G)(A) \to (R^1 G)(J) \to \cdots .
664	  \]

666	  \emph{Killing the acyclic terms.} Because \(J\) is right-\(G\)-acyclic
667	  (Definition~\ref{def:right_acyclic}), every term \((R^k G)(J)\) with \(k \geq 1\)

669	  \[
670	    \underbrace{(R^{k} G)(J)}_{=0} \to (R^{k} G)(Z)
671	      \xrightarrow{\ \delta^k\ } (R^{k+1} G)(A) \to \underbrace{(R^{k+1} G)(J)}_{=0},
672	  \]
673	  has both outer terms zero, so the connecting map
674	  \(\delta^k : (R^{k} G)(Z) \to (R^{k+1} G)(A)\) is an isomorphism, which is the
675	  asserted dimension shift. (The lowest-degree segment of the same long exact
676	  sequence yields the cokernel description of \((R^1 G)(A)\); that reading is
677	  recorded separately in Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_one_iso_coker}.)
678	\end{proof}

751	off those sequences using left-exactness of \(G\). We isolate the two ingredients
